text.title: Prototyping Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every prototyping application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Write learning objectives BEFORE selecting fidelity or tools. Fidelity is chosen by what needs to be learned, never by time available.

rationale.source: IDEO design kit; NNG — prototyping research

rule.rationale: Objectives-first stops cargo-cult fidelity choices and prevents wasted polishing on hypotheses no one tests.

rule.statement: Moderated tests have 3-5 tasks per session. Tasks MUST be scenario-based (situation + goal), never instruction-based (do this, click that).
rule.rationale: Beyond 5 tasks fatigue distorts data; instruction-based tasks invalidate findings by leading participants.

rule.statement: The plan MUST list what is NOT prototyped, with a one-line reason per omission.
rule.rationale: Naming the out-of-scope flow prevents downstream readers from treating absence as endorsement.

rule.statement: Use realistic content (real product names, plausible data). Lorem Ipsum is forbidden because it changes how participants interpret the layout.
rule.rationale: Placeholder content tells participants the design is incomplete and changes their feedback.

rule.statement: A single participant who cannot complete a critical task is a severity-1 finding regardless of sample size. Severity is independent of N.
rule.rationale: Blocker for one means blocker for that population; statistical confidence is a separate concern.

rule.statement: Agent-generated test tasks MUST be reviewed by a human researcher for leading phrasing before participant exposure.
rule.rationale: Agents default to instructional and mildly leading phrasing; participant data collected on unreviewed scripts is invalid.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
